label: R
rationale: R is the primary programming language and analysis environment used throughout the track.

label: Descriptive statistics
rationale: The introductory statistics material implies basic data collection, analysis, and summarization before inference and modeling.

label: Probability
rationale: Probability is explicitly listed as a foundation of the track and supports later inference and modeling topics.

label: Random variables
rationale: Random variables are explicitly named in the probability course and are core to distributions and inference.

label: Probability distributions
rationale: Distributions are explicitly covered in the probability chapter and are central to statistical analysis.

label: Conditional probability
rationale: Conditioning is explicitly mentioned in the probability course and is a key beginner probability idea.

label: Regression analysis
rationale: Regression analysis is explicitly featured in multiple chapters and in the overview as a core statistical skill.

label: Linear regression
rationale: Linear regression is explicitly listed in the intermediate regression chapter and is foundational for predictive modeling.

label: Logistic regression
rationale: Logistic regression is explicitly listed and is a standard beginner classification model.

label: Predictive modeling
rationale: The track emphasizes making predictions and explicitly mentions predictive modeling.

label: Sampling
rationale: Sampling is explicitly a dedicated course and is essential for efficient data collection and inference.

label: Hypothesis testing
rationale: Hypothesis testing is explicitly named as a key skill and has a dedicated chapter.

label: t-test
rationale: t-tests are explicitly listed as part of the hypothesis testing course.

label: Proportion test
rationale: Proportion tests are explicitly named in the hypothesis testing chapter.

label: Chi-square test
rationale: Chi-square tests are explicitly listed as part of the hypothesis testing content.

label: Experimental design
rationale: Experimental design is explicitly named in the overview and has a dedicated chapter.

label: Survey design
rationale: Survey design is explicitly covered in the survey data chapter and overview.

label: Survey data analysis
rationale: The survey data chapter explicitly focuses on analyzing survey results.

label: Data visualization
rationale: Visualizing survey results and creating visualizations with ggplot2 are explicitly mentioned.

label: ggplot2
rationale: ggplot2 is explicitly named as a key R tool for creating visualizations.

label: dplyr
rationale: dplyr is explicitly named as an R tool for data manipulation in the track.

label: Hierarchical models
rationale: Hierarchical models are explicitly named in the dedicated chapter.

label: Mixed effects models
rationale: Mixed effects models are explicitly named and are key methods for grouped data.

label: Random effects
rationale: Random effects are explicitly mentioned as part of fitting hierarchical models.

label: Survival analysis
rationale: Survival analysis is explicitly a dedicated chapter and a major applied statistics topic.

label: Time-to-event data
rationale: The survival analysis chapter explicitly describes working with time-to-event data.

label: Survival models
rationale: Estimating, visualizing, and interpreting survival models is explicitly mentioned.

label: Bayesian data analysis
rationale: Bayesian data analysis is explicitly named in the overview and has a dedicated course.

label: Factor analysis
rationale: Factor analysis is explicitly named in the overview and has a dedicated chapter.

label: Latent variables
rationale: Latent variables are explicitly mentioned in connection with factor analysis.

label: Exploratory factor analysis
rationale: Exploratory factor analysis is explicitly named in the factor analysis chapter.

label: Confirmatory factor analysis
rationale: Confirmatory factor analysis is explicitly named in the factor analysis chapter.

label: Statistical inference
rationale: Inference is explicitly named as a process for drawing conclusions from samples and is central to statistics.

label: Population and sample
rationale: The inference and sampling chapters imply the core distinction between populations and samples.

label: Matplotlib
rationale: Matplotlib is explicitly named in the course title, summary, overview, chapter title, and case study as a core tool for creating visualizations.

label: Dictionary
rationale: The course directly teaches Python dictionaries as an important data structure for storing and accessing data.

label: pandas
rationale: pandas is named repeatedly as a main library in the course for working with tabular data and filtering data.

label: DataFrame
rationale: The pandas DataFrame is presented as a core structure for tabular data manipulation and filtering.

label: Boolean logic
rationale: Boolean logic is explicitly taught as the foundation of decision-making and filtering in Python.

label: Comparison operators
rationale: Comparison operators are specifically mentioned as part of the logic chapter and are essential for conditional statements and filtering.

label: Control flow
rationale: Control flow is explicitly identified as a major course topic used to control decision-making in Python programs.

label: Filtering
rationale: Filtering is directly mentioned as a learner-facing skill, especially for selecting rows in pandas DataFrames using logic.

label: while loop
rationale: The loops chapter explicitly teaches while loops as a way to repeatedly execute code.

label: for loop
rationale: The loops chapter explicitly teaches for loops as a way to iterate over data structures.

label: Random number generation
rationale: The case study uses random number generators to simulate outcomes, making this a concrete applied topic in the course.

label: Hacker statistics
rationale: The final chapter applies course concepts through hacker statistics, which is a recognizable beginner data science simulation method.

label: R
rationale: The course is an introduction to the R programming language and uses it throughout all chapters.

label: Basic data types in R
rationale: The first chapter explicitly introduces basic data types as foundational knowledge for later data structures.

label: Variables in R
rationale: Variable assignment is introduced early as a core beginner programming skill in R.

label: Vectors
rationale: Vectors are a dedicated chapter topic and one of the main basic data structures emphasized in the course.

label: Vector subsetting
rationale: Selecting elements from vectors is a specific learner-facing operation taught in the vectors chapter.

label: Vector comparison
rationale: The course explicitly teaches comparing vectors as a practical operation in R.

label: Matrices
rationale: Matrices are taught in a full chapter and are a key R data structure in the course.

label: Matrix computations
rationale: Basic computations with matrices are explicitly listed as a chapter learning goal.

label: Factors
rationale: Factors are introduced as R's way to store categorical data and are presented as important for data analysis.

label: Categorical data
rationale: The factors chapter frames factors through the broader concept of categorical data.

label: Factor subsetting
rationale: Subsetting factors is explicitly named as a skill taught in the factors chapter.

label: Data frames
rationale: Data frames are a major course topic and are described as the format of most datasets learners will use.

label: Data frame subsetting
rationale: Selecting interesting parts of a data frame is a core practical skill called out in the chapter summary.

label: Ordering data frames
rationale: Ordering a data frame by variables is an explicit learning outcome in the data frames chapter.

label: Lists
rationale: Lists are a dedicated chapter topic and are introduced as a flexible structure that can hold mixed types.

label: List subsetting
rationale: Subsetting lists is explicitly taught as part of working with lists in R.
